facts.text: Rockledge was officially founded on August 7, 1887, making it the oldest incorporated municipality in Brevard County.
The town’s name comes from early settler Cephas Bailey Magruder, who called his home “the rockledge home” after the coquina rock ledges along the Indian River.
In 1888, President Grover Cleveland and his wife visited Rockledge, a highlight in the city’s early history.
Rockledge once hosted several large hotels—Hotel Indian River, the Plaza, and the Rockledge Hotel—catering to Northern tourists escaping winter.
The historic area along Orange, Rockledge, and Rockledge Drive winds along the Indian River and contains about 100 buildings dating back to the late 1800s.
After World War I, the rise of the automobile brought everyday travelers to Rockledge, shifting it from a boat‑and‑rail resort to a more accessible community.
